Apple___Apple_scab.description: Apple scab is caused by the fungus Venturia inaequalis. It causes dark, scaly lesions on leaves, fruit, and twigs.
Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Apply fungicides such as captan or myclobutanil during spring. Remove fallen leaves and infected fruit. Prune trees to improve air circulation.

Apple___Black_rot.description: Black rot is caused by the fungus Botryosphaeria obtusa. It causes brown spots on leaves, rotting fruit, and cankers on branches.
Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Prune dead or diseased branches. Remove mummified fruit. Apply fungicides like captan or thiophanate-methyl.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.description: Caused by Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae. Shows yellow-orange spots on leaves and fruit.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Remove nearby cedar trees if possible. Apply fungicides in spring. Use resistant apple varieties.

Apple___healthy.description: The plant appears to be healthy with no visible signs of disease.
Apple___healthy.treatment: Continue regular care: proper watering, fertilization, and pest monitoring.

Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot.description: Caused by the fungus Cercospora zeae-maydis. Creates rectangular gray-brown lesions on leaves.
Corn___Cercospora_leaf_spot.treatment: Use resistant hybrids. Rotate crops. Apply foliar fungicides like strobilurins or triazoles.

Corn___Common_rust.description: Caused by the fungus Puccinia sorghi. Shows small, circular to elongated brown pustules on leaves.
Corn___Common_rust.treatment: Plant resistant varieties. Apply fungicides if infection is severe. Ensure proper spacing.

Corn___healthy.description: The corn plant appears healthy with no visible disease symptoms.
Corn___healthy.treatment: Maintain regular watering and fertilization schedule. Monitor for pests.

Grape___Black_rot.description: Caused by Guignardia bidwellii. Shows brown circular lesions on leaves and shriveled, black mummified fruit.
Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Remove mummified berries and infected plant parts. Apply fungicides before bloom. Improve air circulation.

Grape___Esca.description: A complex disease caused by multiple fungi. Shows interveinal striping and necrosis on leaves.
Grape___Esca.treatment: Prune and destroy infected wood. Protect pruning wounds. No chemical treatment is fully effective.

Grape___healthy.description: The grape vine appears healthy with no signs of disease.
Grape___healthy.treatment: Continue proper vineyard management: pruning, irrigation, and monitoring.

Potato___Early_blight.description: Caused by Alternaria solani. Shows dark brown spots with concentric rings on lower leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides like chlorothalonil or mancozeb. Rotate crops. Remove infected plant debris.

Potato___Late_blight.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. Shows water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown/black. Can destroy entire crop.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides immediately. Remove and destroy infected plants. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Potato___healthy.description: The potato plant appears healthy with no visible disease symptoms.
Potato___healthy.treatment: Maintain proper watering and hilling. Monitor for pest activity.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.description: Caused by Xanthomonas bacteria. Shows small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves, stems, and fruit.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Use disease-free seeds. Apply copper-based bactericides. Avoid overhead watering. Rotate crops.

Tomato___Early_blight.description: Caused by Alternaria solani. Shows dark spots with concentric rings (target-like) on lower leaves.
Tomato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides. Remove lower infected leaves. Mulch around plants. Practice crop rotation.

Tomato___Late_blight.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. Shows large, water-soaked brown spots on leaves with white mold underneath.
Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Remove infected plants immediately. Apply fungicides. Avoid overhead watering. Use resistant varieties.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.description: Caused by Passalora fulva. Shows yellow spots on upper leaf surface and olive-green mold on lower surface.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Improve ventilation. Reduce humidity. Apply fungicides. Remove infected leaves.

Tomato___healthy.description: The tomato plant appears healthy with no visible disease symptoms.
Tomato___healthy.treatment: Continue regular watering, staking, and monitoring. Ensure adequate sunlight.
